metadata.archetype_justification: The application owns persistent state (SQLite/PostgreSQL databases for music library, configuration, and logs), exposes CRUD operations on business entities (artists, albums, tracks, quality profiles), and manages entity lifecycle (monitored/unmonitored status, import/delete). Classified as stateful-crud.

top_gaps.title: Managed Compute
top_gaps.gap: No managed compute — application distributed as bare-metal packages with no cloud deployment model
top_gaps.impact: Blocks containerization, auto-scaling, and cloud-native modernization

top_gaps.title: Infrastructure as Code Coverage
top_gaps.gap: No IaC exists — all infrastructure is manually provisioned
top_gaps.impact: Prevents automated, reproducible deployments and environment consistency

top_gaps.title: Centralized Identity Integration
top_gaps.gap: Single static API key with no external IdP integration
top_gaps.impact: No SSO, no RBAC, no federated identity — limits multi-user and enterprise adoption

top_gaps.title: Network Security
top_gaps.gap: No VPC, security groups, or network segmentation defined
top_gaps.impact: Application has no cloud networking layer; relies on host-level network config

top_gaps.title: Distributed Tracing
top_gaps.gap: No distributed tracing — only NLog file-based logging and Sentry error tracking
top_gaps.impact: Cannot trace requests across service boundaries; debugging limited to log correlation

quick_agent_wins.name: API-aware Agent
quick_agent_wins.prerequisite: API docs exist (APP-Q5 >= 2) and structured JSON responses detected. OpenAPI 3.0 spec at src/Lidarr.Api.V1/openapi.json.
quick_agent_wins.what_it_enables: An agent that discovers and invokes Lidarr's existing REST API endpoints as tools — managing music libraries, triggering searches, monitoring downloads, and configuring profiles through natural language.
quick_agent_wins.additional_steps: Generate a production-accessible OpenAPI spec for agent tool discovery (currently debug-only).

quick_agent_wins.name: Data Query Agent
quick_agent_wins.prerequisite: Database with clear schema (DATA-Q2 >= 2). Centralized data access layer via BasicRepository pattern with well-defined entity mappings.
quick_agent_wins.what_it_enables: A natural language to SQL agent for querying the music library database — finding artists by criteria, generating collection statistics, identifying missing albums.
quick_agent_wins.additional_steps: Document the database schema (entity relationships from TableMapping.cs) for agent consumption.

quick_agent_wins.name: RAG-based Knowledge Agent
quick_agent_wins.prerequisite: Documentation exists in the repository (README, wiki content, API documentation).
quick_agent_wins.what_it_enables: A knowledge agent that answers user questions about Lidarr configuration, troubleshooting, and usage by indexing existing documentation.
quick_agent_wins.additional_steps: Index the OpenAPI spec, health check documentation, and configuration reference as the knowledge corpus.

decomposition_strategy.rationale: Containerize the monolith first, then selectively extract high-value services. Limited capacity recommended due to desktop application heritage.

findings.title: No Managed Compute
findings.description: All compute is bare-metal/VM-based. Lidarr is distributed as self-contained executables and OS-specific packages with no managed compute services (ECS, EKS, Lambda, Fargate).
findings.gap: No managed compute — no ECS, EKS, Lambda, or Fargate usage. No cloud deployment model exists.
findings.recommendation: Containerize the application using .NET 8.0's native container support and deploy to Amazon EKS (per preferences). The application's environment variable configuration pattern and configurable port binding make it container-ready.

findings.title: Self-Managed Databases
findings.description: Databases are entirely self-managed. SQLite is the default embedded database; PostgreSQL is supported as an optional alternative. Both run on the same host or user-managed infrastructure.
findings.gap: All databases self-managed with no automated failover, no managed backups, and no scaling capability.
findings.recommendation: When moving to cloud deployment, migrate PostgreSQL workloads to Amazon Aurora PostgreSQL (per preferences). Aurora provides automated backups, Multi-AZ failover, and read replica auto-scaling.

findings.title: No External Messaging Infrastructure
findings.description: The application uses an internal event aggregator for in-process pub/sub but no external messaging infrastructure (SQS, SNS, EventBridge, Kafka, RabbitMQ). All event-driven patterns are in-process only.
findings.gap: No external messaging for cross-service state changes. In-process events are lost on restart.
findings.recommendation: When decomposing the monolith, externalize the internal event aggregator to Amazon EventBridge (per preferences). Existing event patterns map directly to EventBridge event types.

findings.title: No Network Security Configuration
findings.description: No VPC, security groups, NACLs, or network segmentation defined. The application binds to a configurable address/port with no cloud networking layer.
findings.gap: No cloud network security — no private subnets, no security groups, no network segmentation.
findings.recommendation: When deploying to AWS, provision within a VPC with private subnets. Place the application behind an ALB or API Gateway in public subnets, with application and database in private subnets.

findings.title: No API Entry Point
findings.description: Services are exposed directly via Kestrel with no gateway, load balancer, or CDN. The application serves both API and frontend SPA on port 8686 with no throttling or WAF.
findings.gap: No API Gateway, ALB, or CloudFront. Direct service exposure with no throttling, no WAF, and no managed TLS termination.
findings.recommendation: Deploy behind Amazon API Gateway for the REST API (with throttling, request validation, and auth) and CloudFront for frontend static assets.

findings.title: No Auto-Scaling
findings.description: No auto-scaling mechanisms configured. The application runs as a single-instance process with no scaling capability.
findings.gap: All capacity is statically provisioned — single instance with no horizontal scaling.
findings.recommendation: When containerized and deployed to EKS, configure Horizontal Pod Autoscaler (HPA) based on CPU/memory and request rate.

findings.title: No Automated Backups
findings.description: No automated backup configuration. SQLite files on local filesystem with no backup automation. Manual backup feature exists but is not scheduled.
findings.gap: No automated backups, no retention policy, no tested restore procedures.
findings.recommendation: When migrated to Aurora PostgreSQL, leverage automated backups with configurable retention and PITR. Configure AWS Backup for cross-region replication.

findings.title: No Infrastructure as Code
findings.description: No IaC exists in the repository. No Terraform, CloudFormation, CDK, Helm charts, or Kubernetes manifests.
findings.gap: 0% IaC coverage — all infrastructure would be manually created.
findings.recommendation: Create IaC using Terraform or CDK to define the AWS infrastructure: EKS cluster, Aurora PostgreSQL, ECR repository, VPC networking, ALB/API Gateway, and monitoring resources.

findings.title: CI/CD Without Deployment Automation
findings.description: Azure Pipelines provides comprehensive build and test automation (multi-platform, integration tests, SonarCloud) but no deployment stage. Artifacts are produced but not deployed.
findings.gap: Build is automated but deployment is entirely manual. No continuous delivery, no environment promotion.
findings.recommendation: Extend CI/CD with deployment stages: build container → push to ECR → deploy to EKS staging → smoke tests → promote to production.

findings.title: Monolith with Identifiable Modules
findings.description: Single deployable unit with 15 projects but shared database access and cross-module coupling. Internal event aggregator provides some decoupling.
findings.gap: Single deployable unit with shared database access across all modules. No independent deployment capability.
findings.recommendation: Begin with Conditional/Adaptive approach: containerize as-is, then extract high-value services (notification dispatch, download management, metadata indexing).

findings.title: Primarily Synchronous Communication
findings.description: Communication is primarily synchronous within the monolith. Internal event aggregator provides fire-and-forget but is in-process only. External service calls are all sync HTTP.
findings.gap: Primarily synchronous with some async for background jobs. No durable async messaging for cross-service state propagation.
findings.recommendation: When decomposing, adopt EventBridge for cross-service state changes. Convert internal event patterns to external events. Use SQS for durable work queues.

findings.title: Long-Running Processes Without Async Infrastructure
findings.description: Long-running operations (library scanning, metadata refresh) run as synchronous background tasks with SignalR progress updates. Command queue provides ordering but blocks until complete.
findings.gap: Some background job processing but inconsistent patterns. No async job infrastructure with status polling.
findings.recommendation: Implement async job processing with Step Functions for complex workflows or SQS + Lambda workers for simple tasks.

findings.title: Limited API Versioning
findings.description: API namespaced under /api/v1/ but only one version exists with no backward compatibility policy or version negotiation.
findings.gap: Versioning applied but only v1 with no policy for v2 introduction or deprecation.
findings.recommendation: Document a versioning strategy: define when breaking changes warrant a new version, implement Accept-Version header support.

findings.title: Static Service Endpoints
findings.description: Service endpoints configured through environment variables and XML config. No dynamic service discovery, no service registry.
findings.gap: Environment variables for endpoints but no dynamic discovery.
findings.recommendation: When deploying to EKS, leverage Kubernetes DNS-based service discovery. Consider AWS Cloud Map for service registration.

findings.title: Local Filesystem Storage
findings.description: Music files, cover art, and metadata stored on local filesystems. No S3 or cloud object storage.
findings.gap: Data in local filesystem with limited accessibility. No cloud object storage, no CDN.
findings.recommendation: Store cover art and metadata in S3 with CloudFront for CDN delivery. Consider S3 File Gateway for media files.

findings.title: Mostly Centralized Data Access
findings.description: Data access mostly centralized through BasicRepository pattern wrapping Dapper. Some direct Dapper access for complex queries.
findings.gap: Mostly centralized with some direct access in auxiliary code paths.
findings.recommendation: Maintain existing repository pattern. When migrating to Aurora, connection strings update with minimal code changes.

findings.title: Database Versions Partially Managed
findings.description: CI/CD tests against PostgreSQL 14 and 15. Npgsql 9.0.3 supports PostgreSQL 13-17. No explicit version pin in deployment config.
findings.gap: Versions not explicitly pinned in deployment. No documented version-update procedure.
findings.recommendation: When deploying to Aurora, pin engine version in IaC. Document upgrade procedure. Plan migration from PG 14 (EOL Nov 2026).

findings.title: API Key Authentication Without Token-Based Auth
findings.description: API authentication uses a single static API key without OAuth2/JWT. Simple string comparison with no expiry, no rate limiting, no per-user identity.
findings.gap: API key authentication without token-based auth. Single shared key grants full access.
findings.recommendation: Implement OAuth2/JWT authentication using Amazon Cognito. Replace static API key with short-lived JWT tokens carrying user identity and scopes.

findings.title: No Centralized Identity Integration
findings.description: Application manages its own authentication entirely with no external IdP. Users stored locally. No OIDC, SAML, or federated identity.
findings.gap: No external IdP integration. No SSO, no federated identity, no centralized user management.
findings.recommendation: Integrate with Amazon Cognito as the identity provider. Implement OIDC authentication flow for the web UI and token-based auth for the API.

findings.title: No Secrets Management Service
findings.description: No plaintext credentials in source but production credentials in environment variables or application config without rotation or centralized management.
findings.gap: Credentials in env vars/config without rotation, encryption, or audit trails. No Secrets Manager or Vault.
findings.recommendation: Store all credentials in AWS Secrets Manager with automated rotation. Use IAM roles for service-to-service auth. Configure RDS integration for automatic password rotation.

findings.title: No Compute Hardening or Patching
findings.description: No compute hardening or patching strategy beyond application self-update. No vulnerability scanning, no hardened images, no OS-level patching.
findings.gap: No patching strategy beyond app self-update. No vulnerability scanning, no hardened images.
findings.recommendation: Use hardened base images (Bottlerocket for EKS, distroless for containers). Enable ECR scanning. Deploy SSM Patch Manager. Integrate vulnerability scanning in CI/CD.

findings.title: Limited Security Pipeline
findings.description: SonarCloud for code quality but no dedicated SAST, no dependency scanning, no container scanning, no security gates.
findings.gap: No dedicated SAST, no dependency scanning (Dependabot/npm audit), no container scanning.
findings.recommendation: Add dependency scanning (Dependabot, npm audit), dedicated SAST (Semgrep/CodeGuru), and container scanning (ECR). Configure blocking gates on critical findings.

findings.title: No Distributed Tracing
findings.description: No distributed tracing instrumented. NLog for logging and Sentry for errors only. No trace ID propagation, no OpenTelemetry, no X-Ray.
findings.gap: No distributed tracing — no trace IDs, no request correlation across boundaries.
findings.recommendation: Integrate OpenTelemetry .NET SDK for distributed tracing. Export to AWS X-Ray. Add W3C TraceContext headers for downstream calls.

findings.title: No Formal SLO Definitions
findings.description: Health checks monitor system state but no formal SLOs defined. No availability targets, latency percentiles, or error budgets.
findings.gap: Basic health checks but no formal SLO definitions or error budget tracking.
findings.recommendation: Define SLOs for critical user journeys: API latency p95 < 500ms, search availability 99.9%, import success rate > 99%.

findings.title: No Business Metrics
findings.description: No custom business metrics published. No metrics framework — no CloudWatch, Prometheus, or StatsD. Only log entries for operational data.
findings.gap: No custom metrics — no business outcome tracking, no operational dashboards.
findings.recommendation: Instrument business metrics using CloudWatch EMF: albums imported/hour, search success rate, download completion rate.

findings.title: No Anomaly Detection or Alerting
findings.description: No anomaly detection or alerting configured. Health checks are binary pass/fail with no threshold-based alerting or escalation.
findings.gap: No alerting — no anomaly detection, no threshold alarms, no escalation policies.
findings.recommendation: Configure CloudWatch Alarms for error rate spikes, latency degradation. Enable Anomaly Detection. Integrate with SNS for escalation.

findings.title: Integration Tests With Minor Gaps
findings.description: Integration tests exist in CI against PostgreSQL 14/15 in Docker. Automation tests provide E2E coverage. Tests run on multiple platforms.
findings.gap: Integration tests for primary workflows; some gaps in comprehensive coverage.
findings.recommendation: Expand integration test coverage to all critical API workflows. Add contract tests for third-party consumers.

findings.title: No Incident Response Automation
findings.description: No runbooks, no incident response automation, no self-healing patterns. Incident handling entirely ad hoc.
findings.gap: No runbooks — no automation, no documented procedures, no self-healing.
findings.recommendation: Create runbooks for common incidents as SSM Automation documents. Implement self-healing for recoverable failures.

findings.title: No Observability Ownership
findings.description: No observability ownership defined. No CODEOWNERS for monitoring, no dashboards, no named alarm owners, no team attribution.
findings.gap: No observability ownership — no dashboards, no named owners, no team attribution.
findings.recommendation: Establish observability ownership: CODEOWNERS for dashboards/alarms, named owners, per-service dashboards with team tags.

detail.current_state: Tightly-coupled monolith with identifiable module boundaries but shared database and single deployment unit

pathways.name: Move to Containers
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: INF-Q1=1, no container definitions found (no Dockerfile in repository)

detail.current_state: Self-contained .NET 8.0 executables for 12+ platforms, no Dockerfile
immediate_actions: Deploy to EKS with Helm chart

pathways.name: Move to Open Source
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: DATA-Q4=4 (no stored procedures). No commercial DB engines detected — SQLite and PostgreSQL are both open source.

triggering_questions.note: No stored procedures or proprietary SQL; threshold not met

pathways.not_triggered_reason: All database engines (SQLite, PostgreSQL) are already open source. No commercial engine licensing costs to eliminate.

pathways.name: Move to Managed Databases
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: INF-Q2=1 meets threshold, but contextual analysis shows databases (SQLite embedded, user-managed PostgreSQL) are self-managed by design for a desktop/self-hosted application. Moving to managed databases requires cloud deployment first (addressed by Move to Containers and Move to Modern DevOps pathways).

triggering_questions.note: Self-managed by design for desktop app; cloud deployment prerequisite not met
Versions approaching EOL but partially managed

pathways.not_triggered_reason: While INF-Q2=1 meets the score threshold, the database is self-managed by design for a self-hosted desktop application. Cloud deployment (Move to Containers pathway) is a prerequisite before managed database migration is actionable. Once containerized and deployed to AWS, Aurora PostgreSQL migration becomes the natural next step.

pathways.name: Move to Managed Analytics
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: No data processing workloads, streaming, ETL, or analytics pipelines detected. Contextual guard prevents trigger.
pathways.not_triggered_reason: No evidence of data processing workloads exists — no streaming, no ETL, no analytics pipelines. The application is a music collection manager, not a data processing system.

pathways.name: Move to Modern DevOps
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: INF-Q10=1 (no IaC), INF-Q11=2 (build automated but no deployment)

detail.current_state: Azure Pipelines for build/test; no IaC; no deployment automation
immediate_actions: Create CDK IaC for AWS infrastructure

pathways.name: Move to AI
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: No AI/agent intent detected in portfolio or service context. Context is 'Music collection manager (*arr suite)' with no AI-related signal terms.
pathways.not_triggered_reason: No AI/agent intent detected in portfolio or service context.
